summary.short_rationale: PashaZloy is overlap-heavy, especially against Neuraldeep, but adds strong implementation depth for local LLM infra, open-source agent tooling, MCP/ACP/OpenAPI wrappers, hardware setups, and BDD/TDD vibe-coding practice.
summary.strongest_argument_for: The passport shows concrete reusable artifacts and patterns: Docker/vLLM/llama.cpp local setups, OpenAPI-to-MCP/CLI tooling, SGR/agent orchestration, and tests as long-term memory for agent-assisted development.
summary.strongest_argument_against: All candidate cells overlap existing accepted coverage, with no clean gap and Neuraldeep as the closest existing expert.

accepted_basis_cells.interpretation: local/on-prem LLM setup and hardware-depth complement to existing inference-cost coverage
MCP/ACP/OpenAPI tooling depth; overlaps Neuraldeep but adds implementation artifacts

good_fit: local LLM deployment on consumer GPUs
MCP, ACP, CLI tools, REST wrappers, and agent tool exposure
poor_fit: primary general infra routing when Neuraldeep is better suited

risk_notes: Strong open-source/local-first bias may underweight managed/cloud/vendor options.
Highly technical source; poor fit for beginner or executive audiences.
Fast-moving tooling claims may age quickly.
recommended_manifest_caveat: accepted as local-LLM and open-source agent-tooling depth expert; heavy overlap with Neuraldeep, avoid broad duplicate routing

probe_shape: 1 vibe-coding/TDD query where PashaZloy should be supporting
